name: Deadlock (prevention & detection)
def: A state where threads wait on each other cyclically so none can proceed.
problem: Avoid and recover from circular waits that permanently freeze concurrent systems.
mechanism: Prevent by breaking one Coffman condition — e.g. global lock ordering, try-lock with backoff, or no hold-and-wait; detect via wait-for-graph cycle checks.
benefits: Lock ordering prevents most deadlocks cheaply
limits: Global lock order is hard to maintain at scale
failures: Holding a lock across a callback that re-locks
whenNot: Lock-free or message-passing designs sidestep it
myths: "Deadlocks only involve locks" — they also arise with channels, threads-on-pools and resources
